title: Operating Systems and Software
description: How an operating system manages hardware, processes and memory, how it responds to interrupts, and how systems, application and utility software differ.
objectives: Explain the core purpose of an operating system: managing hardware resources, providing a user interface, and running and managing processes
Describe the states a process passes through and trace round-robin scheduling given process burst times and a time slice
Explain how paging and segmentation implement virtual memory, and compare their advantages
Distinguish systems software, utility software and application software, and classify examples of each
Explain what an interrupt is and describe how the fetch-decode-execute cycle checks for one at the end of every cycle
